locations.name: 约翰·爱德华·格雷

relations.subject: 欧洲小雨燕
relations.evidence: The little swift (Apus affinis) is a small species of swift found in Africa and southwestern Asia, and are vagrants and local breeders in southern Europe

relations.subject: 欧洲小雨燕
relations.evidence: They are found both in urban areas and at rocky cliffs where they build nests in a way typical of all members of the order Apodiformes
relations.object_summary: 大型猛禽和食腐鸟偏好的高崖栖息环境。

relations.subject: 欧洲小雨燕
relations.evidence: This species has a wide distribution and its population is increasing, and as a result is classified as "Least Concern" by the International Union for Conservation of Nature and Natural Resources. The population of the species is expanding and increasing, mainly due to its tendency to build nests in man-made structures. However, in Turkey a local decline of about 50% was observed due to habitat loss resulting from dam construction
relations.object_summary: 开发、采伐和围垦等活动导致适宜生境减少。
